People think of design teams as very exclusive, but you should invite everybody for a conversation. However, ideas are truly fragile and you can't just go around poking holes in them. You also have to have thought about the idea deeply enough to be able to have a discussion at a similar frequency wavelength. If you're just shallowly thinking of ideas, it's unfair to expect everyone to bring you into the discussion because you haven't thought deeply enough. Whether you're a designer or an engineer, that doesn't matter. What matters is whether you've thought deeply enough, because it's a sign of respect when you're truly dissecting an idea. You have done your homework, figured it out, thought about it, and have an original point of view to bring to the conversation. Then who cares if you're a designer, engineer, or someone else? I don't care. You just need to have thought about it. That's another way of protecting the creative energy, because it's very easy to not have that protected. I've seen designers get burnt out, some switch from design to never being a designer again—they become engineers. It needs to be protected. Apple is really good at that; they keep all designers in an ivory castle. Airbnb is also pretty good at preserving the creative energy of the design team. I'm sure other world-class design teams also make it a core aspect of how they allow people to flourish: by preserving the thing that gives you all your good ideas. That's how you come up with good stuff. You have to preserve it like any other resource.
turns.zh: 我会不惜一切代价保护他们的创作精神、创作能量，并将其引导出来。你仍然需要给予反馈等等，但创作能量需要在某些方面得到保护和维护。人们认为设计团队非常排外，但你应该邀请每个人参与对话。然而，想法确实很脆弱，你不能随便在上面戳洞。你还必须对想法有足够深入的思考，才能在一个相近的频率波长上进行讨论。如果你只是肤浅地思考想法，那么期望每个人都把你带入讨论是不公平的，因为你没有足够深入地思考。无论你是设计师还是工程师，这都不重要。重要的是你是否足够深入地思考过，因为当你真正剖析一个想法时，这是尊重的表现。你已经做了功课，弄清楚了，思考过了，并且带来了一个原创的观点。那么谁在乎你是设计师、工程师还是其他人？我不在乎。你只需要思考过。这是保护创作能量的另一种方式，因为不保护它很容易。我见过设计师精疲力竭，有些人从设计转行，再也不做设计师了——他们成了工程师。它需要被保护。苹果在这方面做得很好，他们把设计师都留在象牙塔里。Airbnb 也很擅长保护设计团队的创作能量。我相信其他世界级的设计团队也把这一点作为让人们蓬勃发展的核心：保护那个能给你所有好想法的东西。这就是你如何想出好东西。你必须像保护任何其他资源一样保护它。

transcript.secZh: 保护设计团队的创意能量

transcript.sec: Advice for those not yet immersed in AI

turns.en: We've covered a ton of ground. Before I let you go, I want to give one more generic catch-all to make sure we're hitting everything you want to talk about. Maybe we could address the person listening who is inspired by everything you're saying, has been keeping tabs on some AI stuff, but isn't at a forward-looking company and none of it is really seeping into their day-to-day yet. Any advice or learnings you want to share with that person?
turns.zh: 我们覆盖了很多内容。在你离开之前，我想再做一个通用的总结，确保我们涵盖了你所有想谈的话题。也许我们可以对听众说几句，他们被你所说的一切所启发，一直在关注一些 AI 的东西，但他们不在一个前瞻性的公司，这些东西还没有真正渗透到他们的日常工作中。你有什么建议或经验想分享给那个人吗？

turns.en: If you're in the Twitter sphere, you'll hear that taste is all that matters, high agency is all that matters. I think agency is really important. When you don't have agency, you become pessimistic and snarky. But when you don't have enough agency, you also start lacking curiosity. So I would say don't go after high agency. That's not something you go after. You can cultivate it, but maybe start by just being curious. If you're at a place where AI isn't at the forefront, not every place needs to be. There was definitely a trend of everyone jumping into AI because it was the thing to do. Maybe you're lucky you're not at a company like that. Your company is probably more intentional. So the first question to answer is: are you drawn by it? Is anything here interesting to you? Because if it's not, you're just chasing another meme cycle. It's like people who got into crypto because they thought it would make them rich, not because they were fundamentally allured by the idea of shared ownership. For me, that was the most exciting part about crypto. For AI, first, is it intriguing to you? Is there any part that excites you, mildly scares you, but also interests you? Second, if it's not something you can see, there are small ways you can use it in your day-to-day life. If you're a designer, using things like Cursor, Lovable, v0 for quick tiny prototypes is very easy. Even if you work at Apple with NDAs galore, you can probably find ways to do this. You're already ahead of many people because you're not just tweeting about it; you're actually experiencing it and understanding what works and what the limitations are. This can be true for text-to-language prototyping, coding, text-to-image, text-to-video. If you're a creative person, see if text-to-image or text-to-video can bring a new approach to your creative practice. Could this be the beginning of something you've always wanted? One thing I've always wanted to do is a fashion line, so for me, these tools are a quick way to tap into that seed of practice while commuting or watching something. That's another way to do this. Lastly, you can always change your job—wink wink, we are hiring. But before that, there are so many side projects you can do. One I saw recently was a podcast where a person and an AI just talk. I thought, "That's so cool." The field is super fresh, barely two years old. Going back to 2007, you're at best at 2009. Most interesting companies didn't even start at that point. So there's so much left to do. Follow things you're naturally curious about and see if there are parts that excite you. You don't have to learn about everything—agents, multimodels, and all the other 50 things in your Twitter feed. Pick the one or two that actually excite you and go deeper on them, and see what form it takes.
turns.zh: 如果你在推特圈子里，你会听到品味最重要，高能动性最重要。我认为能动性确实很重要。当你没有能动性时，你会变得悲观和尖刻。但当你能动性不足时，你也会开始缺乏好奇心。所以我会说，不要追求高能动性。那不是你可以直接追求的东西。你可以培养它，但也许从保持好奇心开始。如果你在一个 AI 不是前沿的地方，不是每个地方都需要是前沿。确实有一阵风潮，每个人都跳进 AI，因为那是该做的事。也许你很幸运不在那样的公司。你的公司可能更有意图。所以第一个要回答的问题是：你被它吸引吗？这里有什么让你感兴趣吗？因为如果不是，你只是在追逐另一个 meme 周期。就像那些进入加密货币领域的人，因为他们认为这会让他们致富，而不是因为他们被共享所有权的想法所吸引。对我来说，那是加密货币最令人兴奋的部分。对于 AI，首先，它让你好奇吗？有没有任何部分让你兴奋、有点害怕，但也感兴趣？其次，如果你看不到它，你可以在日常生活中找到小方法使用它。如果你是设计师，使用像 Cursor、Lovable、v0 这样的工具来快速制作小型原型非常容易。即使你在苹果工作，NDA 满天飞，你也能找到方法做到这一点。你已经领先很多人了，因为你不仅仅在发推文，你实际上在体验它，理解什么有效、什么有限制。这对于文本到语言的原型设计、编码、文本到图像、文本到视频都是如此。如果你是一个有创造力的人，看看文本到图像或文本到视频能否为你的创作实践带来新方法。这可能是你一直想要的东西的开始吗？我一直想做的一件事是时装系列，所以对我来说，这些工具是在通勤或观看东西时快速触及那个实践种子的方式。这是另一种做法。最后，你总是可以换工作—— wink wink，我们在招聘。但在此之前，你可以做很多副项目。我最近看到一个项目，是一个人和一个 AI 一起做播客聊天。我觉得“这太酷了”。这个领域非常新，才两年左右。回到 2007 年，你最多在 2009 年。大多数有趣的公司那时甚至还没开始。所以还有很多事情可以做。追随你自然好奇的东西，看看有没有让你兴奋的部分。你不需要学习所有东西——智能体、多模态模型以及你推特信息流中的其他 50 件事。选择一两个真正让你兴奋的，深入下去，看看它会变成什么形式。

turns.en: Maybe it'll actually lead to your next job, or maybe you will actually convince the company you're at, because by building these, you show them the value of what this domain can bring. Both of these in my head are things that high agency people would do, instead of just saying, 'Oh, my company doesn't let me do it.' It's like, so who's stopping you? That's kind of my advice.
turns.zh: 也许它真的会带来你的下一份工作，或者你可能会说服你所在的公司，因为通过构建这些，你向他们展示了这个领域能带来的价值。在我看来，这两件事都是高自主性的人会做的，而不是只说“哦，我公司不让我做”。问题是，谁在阻止你？这就是我的建议。

turns.en: I love it. I love it. Well dude, this has been really really fun. Thanks for coming on today and sharing a little bit about what you think and what you're working on. I'm really enjoying just following along with your journey and the products so far. So, appreciate your time.
turns.zh: 我喜欢这个。太棒了。伙计，今天真的非常开心。感谢你来做客，分享你的想法和你正在做的事情。我一直很享受关注你的旅程和目前的产品。所以，感谢你的时间。

turns.en: Thank you and thank you for having me.
turns.zh: 谢谢你，也谢谢你邀请我。

turns.en: Before I let you go, I want to take just one minute to run you through my favorite products because I'm constantly asked what's in my stack. Framer is how I build websites. Genway is how I do research. Granola is how I take notes during crit. Jitter is how I animate my designs. Lovable is how I build my ideas in code. Mobin is how I find design inspiration. Paper is how I design like a creative. And Raycast is my shortcut every step of the way. Now, I've hand selected these companies so that I can do these episodes full-time. So, by far the number one way to support the show is to check them out. You can find the full list at dive.comclub/partners.
turns.zh: 在你走之前，我想花一分钟介绍一下我最喜欢的产品，因为我经常被问到我的工具栈是什么。Framer 是我建网站的工具。Genway 是我做研究的方式。Granola 是我在评审时记笔记的工具。Jitter 是我为设计做动画的工具。Lovable 是我用代码实现想法的工具。Mobin 是我寻找设计灵感的地方。Paper 是我像创意人一样设计的工具。而 Raycast 是我每一步的快捷方式。我精心挑选了这些公司，这样我就能全职做这些节目了。所以，支持这个节目的首要方式就是去看看它们。你可以在 dive.comclub/partners 找到完整列表。
